perturbation.original_text: In exchange for the Share Consideration, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual and non-perpetual license (subject to the duration and scope, and format limitations for which Licensor has the rights to each Title as specified in Schedule A1-A6 of Schedule A) to: i. license, exhibit, distribute, reproduce, transmit, perform, display, and otherwise exploit and make available each Title within the Territory in any language by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD) for Internet, TV and mobile platforms (including, but not limited to, OTT streaming services, Sites and Mobile Sites), subject to these limitations for each of the Title in Schedule A1-A6:
perturbation.changed_text: In exchange for the Share Consideration, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee a non-exclusive, royalty-bearing, terminable license (subject to the duration and scope, and format limitations for which Licensor has the rights to each Title as specified in Schedule A1-A6 of Schedule A) to: i. license, exhibit, distribute, reproduce, transmit, perform, display, and otherwise exploit and make available each Title within the Territory in any language by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD) for Internet, TV and mobile platforms (including, but not limited to, OTT streaming services, Sites and Mobile Sites), subject to these limitations for each of the Title in Schedule A1-A6:
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the License is "royalty-free, perpetual and non-perpetual". The modified text changes this to "royalty-bearing, terminable." This creates a direct contradiction because the original text specifies "royalty-free" and "perpetual" which is directly negated by "royalty-bearing" and "terminable" respectively. This contradiction introduces uncertainty in the enforcement because it is unclear whether the Licensee owes royalties or if the license can be terminated, contradicting the idea that it is perpetual.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No royalty or fees of any kind shall be owed by Licensee under this Agreement. The consideration for the licenses granted by Licensor to Licensee under this Agreement is the issuance of the IP Common Shares as defined in the Amended and Securities Purchase Agreement, dated as of December 21, 2015, by and among the Licensee and the Licensor (the "Share Consideration").
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text specified a "royalty-free" license, while the changed text states "royalty-bearing." This directly contradicts the "No royalty or fees of any kind shall be owed by Licensee" clause in the contradicted text. Similarly, the original text implied a perpetual license, but the changed text states it is "terminable", which contradicts the perpetual nature of the license.

perturbation.original_text: Licensor will use its partners and media channels to expand distribution of Licensee's VOD services to more cable MSOs and all other platforms for which Licensee is permitted to distribute the Titles under Section 2(a)(i) [License Grant].
perturbation.changed_text: Licensor will use its partners and media channels to expand distribution of Licensee's online video services to more cable MSOs and all other platforms for which Licensee is permitted to distribute the Titles under Section 2(a)(i) [License Grant].
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies "VOD services". The modified text changes this to "online video services." While seemingly similar, the term VOD (Video On Demand) is specifically defined in section 1(q), while 'online video services' is not defined anywhere in the agreement. This lack of clarity could lead to disputes over whether certain services fall under the scope of the agreement. The contradiction resides in section 2, where Licensee has specific grants to the use of VOD but now that the term changed, the specific grants are unlcear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In exchange for the Share Consideration, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee a non-exclusive, royalty-bearing, terminable license (subject to the duration and scope, and format limitations for which Licensor has the rights to each Title as specified in Schedule A1-A6 of Schedule A) to: i. license, exhibit, distribute, reproduce, transmit, perform, display, and otherwise exploit and make available each Title within the Territory in any language by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD) for Internet, TV and mobile platforms (including, but not limited to, OTT streaming services, Sites and Mobile Sites), subject to these limitations for each of the Title in Schedule A1-A6:
perturbation.justification: The change from 'VOD services' to 'online video services' creates a contradiction because 'VOD' is specifically defined in section 1(q), providing a clear scope, while 'online video services' is not. This undefined term impacts Section 2, where the Licensee is granted rights specifically to 'VOD' (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD). By changing the term to an undefined 'online video services', the agreement creates an ambiguity and affects the licensee's specific granted rights. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall defend, indemnify and hold the other party and its Affiliates, and their respective directors, officers, employees, agents, successors, assigns, licensees and distributors harmless from and against any and all judgments, settlements, damages, penalties, costs and expenses (including, but not limited to, reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of any third party claims relating to the Indemnifying Party's breach or alleged breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall defend, indemnify and hold the other party and its Affiliates, and their respective directors, officers, employees, agents, successors, assigns, and distributors harmless from and against any and all judgments, settlements, damages, penalties, costs and expenses (including, but not limited to, reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of any third party claims relating to the Indemnifying Party's breach or alleged breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original text includes "licensees" within the list of entities to be indemnified. The modified version omits "licensees." This creates a contradiction because it is no longer explicit that licensees are covered under the indemnification clause. This uncertainty leads to conflict when a licensee is affected by a third-party claim. Now it is a question whether or not they are protected under indemnification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Indemnified Party will reasonably cooperate (at the Indemnifying Party's expense) in the defense of any claim for which the Indemnified Party seeks indemnification under this Section 13. The Indemnified Party shall have the right to employ separate counsel and to participate in (but not control) any such action, but the fees and expenses of such counsel will be at the expense of the Indemnified Party unless: (i) the employment of counsel by the Indemnified Party has been authorized by the Indemnifying Party; (ii) the Indemnified Party has been advised by its counsel in writing that there is a conflict of interest between the Indemnifying Party and the Indemnified Party in the conduct of the defense of the action (in which case the Indemnifying Party will not have the right to direct the defense of the action on behalf of the Indemnified Party); or (iii) the Indemnifying Party has not in fact employed counsel to assume the defense of the action within a reasonable time following receipt of the notice given pursuant to this Section 13, in each of which cases the fees and expenses of such counsel will be at the expense of the Indemnifying Party. The Indemnifying Party will not be liable for any settlement of an action effected without its written consent (which consent will not be unreasonably withheld or delayed), nor will the Indemnifying Party settle any such action without the written consent of the Indemnified Party (which consent will not be unreasonably withheld or delayed). The Indemnifying Party will not consent to the entry of any judgment or enter into any settlement that does not include as an unconditional term thereof the giving by the claimant or plaintiff to the Indemnified Party a release from all liability with respect to the claim.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes 'licensees' within the list of entities to be indemnified. The changed text omits 'licensees.' The contradicted text discusses the process for the Indemnified Party to seek indemnification. By removing 'licensees' from the indemnification clause in the changed text, it is now unclear if licensees are covered under the indemnification clause. This creates a direct conflict because if a licensee seeks indemnification, it is uncertain whether they are entitled to it under Section 13, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
